title: They really launched this tiny spacecraft (Well, sort of.) | Space photo of the day for May 11, 2026
summary: A tiny spacecraft was launched, marking the smallest Mars test mission to date. The event highlights advancements in miniaturized space technology.

title: Sun unleashes colossal solar flare and coronal mass ejection, raising the chances of northern lights this week
summary: The Sun emitted an M5.7 solar flare and a coronal mass ejection, causing radio blackouts over the Atlantic and increasing the likelihood of northern lights this week.

title: See Artemis 2's amazing views of Earth in timelapse video taken from 12,000-photo drop
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning timelapse video of Earth from over 12,000 photos during their historic mission, marking the first human moon mission in over 50 years.

title: New James Webb Space Telescope images reveal how massive star clusters can reshape galaxies
summary: New images from the James Webb Space Telescope reveal how massive star clusters can reshape galaxies. The findings provide stunning insights into galactic evolution.

title: Does Dante's Inferno from the 14th century depict an asteroid impact?
summary: A recent analysis suggests that Dante's description of Lucifer's fall in 'Inferno' may depict an asteroid impact, featuring a multi-ringed crater with a central peak. This interpretation links medieval literature to modern impact crater features.

title: 'Pragmata' director Yonghee Cho on creating Capcom’s resonant sci-fi lunar adventure (interview)
summary: Yonghee Cho, director of the film 'Pragmata', discusses the creation of Capcom's sci-fi lunar adventure game. The project blends innovative storytelling with the challenges of game development.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 209 — Astronauts for America
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' features a discussion with Garrett Reisman and Steve Lindsey, leaders of the new organization 'Astronauts for America'. The podcast episode focuses on their initiatives and perspectives.

title: NASA's twin Voyager spacecraft are very low on power after nearly 50 years. How long can they keep going?
summary: NASA's Voyager spacecraft, launched in 1977, are running low on power after nearly 50 years in interstellar space. Their remaining lifespan is uncertain as power continues to dwindle.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 reattempts WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 will reattempt a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful 33-engine static fire test. The event is part of ongoing preparations for a future launch.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also making strides in deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion as Flight 12 approaches, six months after the conclusion of Flight 11. The old design of Pad 1 is being updated to support upcoming missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 launch, potentially marking a calm period before an upcoming surge in activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight with a revised trajectory. The return of Starship launches is expected within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, experienced in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The mission is part of NASA's ongoing efforts to maintain a human presence in low Earth orbit.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant milestone for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in the space sector.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive space-related technology or component. The image accompanying the news highlights its robustness and precision.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical testing phase for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out'. This process likely involves thermal testing to ensure the parachute's reliability under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will aid the rover's landing in 2028 to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for its navigation system. The exact location of the signal reception was not specified in the provided text.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype sovereign intelligence platform for tactical EO imagery, enhancing troops' access to geospatial data. The project aims to provide quicker and easier access to critical imagery for military operations.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round, unveiling a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for a sustained US presence on the Moon.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, rebranding it as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million through an IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. This follows their S-1 filing on May 1, which had set a price range of $24 to $26 per share.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging satellites as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to enhance EO capabilities with advanced thermal data.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is aiming to commercialize quantum research, positioning itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is focusing on advancing quantum technologies to drive economic and scientific growth.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy. The company aims to mitigate fuel line vulnerabilities by harnessing solar power day and night.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a funding round, including a $300 million Series E led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton. The company's valuation reached $2.8 billion.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: UPCOMING: From Perk to Platform: How In-Flight Connectivity Is Rewriting Aviation Economics
summary: A joint webinar by Aviation Tech Today and Via Satellite discusses how in-flight connectivity has evolved from a passenger amenity to a core aircraft infrastructure, significantly impacting aviation economics.
company: Aviation Tech Today and Via Satellite

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. This deal marks a significant agreement for Planet in the European market.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimentation to operational use, facing challenges due to limited data. The shift highlights the need for scalable solutions to sustain advancements.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 amid its ongoing acquisition process by Amazon. The satellite operator's financial performance reflects its strategic positioning during the deal.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026 and highlighted progress on its Neutron rocket, a robotics acquisition, and an SBI deal.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The Indian launch startup is a rising star in the country's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after pricing its shares at the high end of expectations.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites per year. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported a 40% year-over-year revenue growth in its satellite manufacturing business for Q1 2026, driven by projects for Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley clarified that the Globalstar deal did not impact these financial results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to meet evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is prioritizing resilience and adoption of emerging technologies.
